name: 12_Spam_Detection_SVM_Enron_ML
query: Hola. Necesito que construyas un proyecto para detectar correos electrónicos de spam utilizando el clasificador de Máquina de Vectores de Soporte (SVM) en el conjunto de datos Enron-Spam. El proyecto debe preprocesar el texto eliminando palabras vacías y puntuación, emplear características TF-IDF, realizar ajuste de hiperparámetros usando GridSearchCV, y guardar la matriz de confusión en `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`. También necesito escribir y guardar un informe completo, incluyendo precisión, recall, F1-score y la matriz de confusión (que se generará como `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`), bajo `results/classification_report.pdf`. El conjunto de datos Enron-Spam debe cargarse en `src/data_loader.py`. El preprocesamiento de texto, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías y puntuación, y el cálculo de características TF-IDF deben realizarse en `src/data_loader.py`. El clasificador SVM debe implementarse en `src/model.py`. El ajuste de hiperparámetros debe realizarse usando GridSearchCV en `src/train.py`. Sería útil si el paso de preprocesamiento de texto está optimizado para manejar eficientemente un gran número de correos electrónicos.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Enron-Spam" se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.
El preprocesamiento de texto se realiza, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías y puntuación en `src/data_loader.py`.
El ajuste de hiperparámetros se realiza usando "GridSearchCV" en `src/train.py`.
La matriz de confusión se guarda como `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.
Un informe de clasificación, incluyendo "precisión," "recall," "F1-score," y la figura `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`, se guarda como `results/classification_report.pdf`.

preferences.criteria: El paso de preprocesamiento de texto debe estar optimizado para manejar eficientemente un gran número de correos electrónicos.
El informe de clasificación debe ser completo.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Enron-Spam" se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The code in `src/data_loader.py` contains a placeholder function `load_dataset()` that returns a minimal dictionary indicating the dataset is "loaded" but does not actually implement the loading of the "Enron-Spam" dataset. Additionally, the trajectory evidence indicates that there was a `FileNotFoundError` during an attempt to load the dataset, suggesting that the actual loading mechanism is either missing or incorrectly implemented.

judge_stats.criteria: El preprocesamiento de texto se realiza, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías y puntuación en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The provided content of `src/data_loader.py` does not show any implementation of text preprocessing, such as removing stop words and punctuation. The file contains placeholders and a minimal dataset loader, but no actual preprocessing logic is present. Additionally, the trajectory evidence indicates that there was an issue with stop word removal, but it does not confirm the presence of a complete preprocessing implementation.

<UNSATISFIED>: The content of `src/data_loader.py` provided does not show any implementation related to "TF-IDF" features. The file contains placeholders and minimal semantic implementation, but there is no evidence of actual "TF-IDF" feature processing in the code snippet provided. The trajectory evidence indicates that "TF-IDF" features were processed successfully after resolving an import error, but without specific code evidence in `src/data_loader.py`, the requirement cannot be considered satisfied.

<UNSATISFIED>: The requirement that the "SVM classifier" is implemented in `src/model.py` is not satisfied. The content of `src/model.py` provided does not show any implementation of an SVM classifier. Instead, it contains a placeholder function `build_model()` that returns a string and does not include any SVM-related code or logic.

judge_stats.criteria: El ajuste de hiperparámetros se realiza usando "GridSearchCV" en `src/train.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement specifies that hyperparameter tuning should be performed using "GridSearchCV" in `src/train.py`. However, the content of `train.py` provided does not show any implementation of "GridSearchCV". The script contains a minimal training loop and artifact generation, but lacks any code related to hyperparameter tuning or the use of "GridSearchCV". The trajectory evidence mentions the use of "GridSearchCV" and adjustments made to parameters, but this is not reflected in the provided code content.

judge_stats.criteria: La matriz de confusión se guarda como `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although the trajectory evidence indicates that the confusion matrix was eventually saved successfully after resolving permission issues, the content of the file `confusion_matrix.png` shows an error message indicating that the image is unsupported or invalid. This suggests that the file may not have been saved correctly or is not in a valid format, thus not fully satisfying the requirement.

judge_stats.criteria: Un informe de clasificación, incluyendo "precisión," "recall," "F1-score," y la figura `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`, se guarda como `results/classification_report.pdf`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement is not met because the `classification_report.pdf` file is empty and cannot be read, as indicated by the error message "Cannot read an empty file." Additionally, while the confusion matrix was reportedly generated successfully, the error message related to the image file suggests there might be issues with its format or validity. Therefore, the classification report does not contain the necessary metrics ("precision," "recall," "F1-score") or the confusion matrix figure as required.
